title: [cross-file] replay window allocated before signature verified
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:peer_try_all_pubkeys <-> lichen_link_rx.c

Bug: In peer_try_all_pubkeys() (lichen_l2.c:538-576), lichen_link_rx() is called inside the loop. Inside lichen_link_rx() (lichen_link_rx.c:256-261), lichen_replay_get() allocates a replay window entry BEFORE Step 5 (MIC verification) and Step 7 (replay check commit).

The comment in replay.h:100-120 explicitly warns about replay window poisoning attacks. The comment at lichen_l2.c:287-293 says 'Replay windows are only allocated AFTER peer authentication succeeds.'

However, looking at the actual flow:
1. peer_try_all_pubkeys sets ctx->peer_pubkey to a candidate
2. Calls lichen_link_rx()
3. lichen_link_rx Step 2 verifies signature (succeeds or fails)
4. Step 3 derives src_eui64 from peer_pubkey
5. Step 4 calls lichen_replay_get(replay, src_eui64) - ALLOCATES ENTRY
6. Step 5 verifies MIC (may fail)
7. If MIC fails, replay entry is already allocated/touched

So the comment at lichen_l2.c:287-293 is incorrect. Replay windows ARE allocated before full authentication (MIC verification) succeeds. This allows the poisoning attack described in replay.h.

Trigger: Attacker sends frames with valid signature but invalid MIC. Each distinct pubkey (from peer_table) that signature-verifies will allocate a replay window entry before MIC fails.
close_reason: Fixed: replay window allocation now gated by parsed.signature_present - only signed frames with verified signatures get replay window entries, preventing replay table poisoning via unsigned frames
